Newsroom

Learn more about the work of the Annie E. Casey Foundation by reviewing our latest news releases.

New KIDS COUNT Index Reveals Where States Are Making Progress for Children

Posted June 8, 2026

Explore the 2026 KIDS COUNT Data Book and new state scores that track child well-being across education, health, economic well-being and family life.

Child Poverty Nearly Triples to 13% Over Three Years

Posted October 20, 2025

U.S. child poverty nearly tripled to 13% in 2024, a new Annie E. Casey Foundation report finds, showing public policies can cut the rate by half.

U.S. States See Progress, Setbacks in Child Well-Being in 2025 Report

Posted June 9, 2025

The 2025 KIDS COUNT Data Book measures child well-being across four domains using 16 key indicators. Explore the full report.

Casey Foundation Names Allison Gerber Vice President for the Center for Economic Opportunity

Posted December 4, 2024

The Foundation has named Allison Gerber the new vice president of its Center for Economic Opportunity. Read the announcement.

Pandemic Learning Loss and COVID-19: Education Impacts

Posted June 10, 2024

Learn about the negative impact of COVID-19 on education. Explore key findings on learning loss resulting from the pandemic today.

Despite Improvements Since 2019, Racial Inequities Persist in Atlanta, Report Says

Posted May 2, 2024

The Casey Foundation’s 2024 Changing the Odds shows Black Atlantans continue to lack access to opportunities. Learn more about the report's findings.

Every State Is Failing to Equip All Kids for Success, Especially Children of Color, Says New Report

Posted January 10, 2024

Race for Results identifies gaps in child well-being that persist in every state across race and ethnicity. Read the press release.

The Impact of the High Cost of Child Care

Posted June 14, 2023

Learn about how the high cost of child care hurts families and the economy. Explore the obstacles created by inaccessible and unaffordable child care.

Report: Children Make Up Smallest-Ever Share of U.S. Population

Posted April 3, 2023

Children accounted for just 22% of the country’s total population in 2020, an all-time low, according to an analysis released by Casey Foundation.

Xander Perry to Succeed May Ng as Chief Investment Officer

Posted March 13, 2023

Xander Perry succeeds May Ng as vice president and chief investment officer at the Annie E. Casey Foundation. Learn more.

1 in 10 Young Adults, 1 in 30 Kids Experience Homelessness Each Year

Posted March 8, 2023

"Preventing and Ending Youth Homelessness in America" calls for urgent action to address the homelessness crisis affecting millions of young people.

National, State-by-State Data Show Depth of Mental Health Pandemic for Youth

Posted August 8, 2022

Children in America are in the midst of a mental health crisis, struggling with anxiety and depression. Explore state-by-state data.

Foundation Welcomes Twelfth Class of Children and Family Fellows

Posted February 25, 2022

The Casey Foundation has selected 15 leaders for the twelfth class of its Children and Family Fellowship. Read who is in the new class.

50-State Data Report Confirms Urgent Need to Make Game-Changing Expanded Child Tax Credit Permanent

Posted June 21, 2021

As a new federal site aids child tax credit access, the Casey Foundation’s report urges making the expansion permanent. See state-by-state data.

The Annie E. Casey Foundation Names Stephen Plank Vice President of Research, Evaluation, Evidence and Data

Posted May 13, 2021

The Annie E. Casey Foundation has named Stephen Plank its new vice president of Research, Evaluation, Evidence and Data.

National Partnership to Reinvent Child Welfare Expands

Posted April 22, 2021

Thriving Families, Safer Children: A National Commitment to Well-Being includes child welfare jurisdictions in 22 states and a sovereign tribal nation.

The Annie E. Casey Foundation Names Patrice Cromwell Vice President, Center for Economic Opportunity

Posted February 16, 2021

The Annie E. Casey Foundation has named Patrice Cromwell as vice president for its Center for Economic Opportunity.

New Report Finds Many Families with Children Are Depressed, Uninsured, Hungry and at Risk of Foreclosure or Eviction

Posted December 14, 2020

A Casey Foundation report urges policy action as COVID-19 left millions struggling with finances, school, work and mental health. Learn more.

First-of-its-Kind Partnership Aims to Redesign Child Welfare Into Child and Family Well-Being Systems

Posted September 9, 2020

Thriving Families, Safer Children shifts from reactive child protection to family support, preventing maltreatment and unnecessary separations. Read more.

The Annie E. Casey Foundation Releases 2020 KIDS COUNT Data Book

Posted June 22, 2020

The Casey Foundation’s 2020 KIDS COUNT Data Book tracks and ranks child well-being nationwide. Learn why it was released during COVID-19.

View All News Releases

Media Contact

Strategic Communications
The Annie E. Casey Foundation
[email protected]

  • Meet Our Experts

Learn About the Baltimore News Collaborative

Supported by the Casey Foundation, the Baltimore News Collaborative is a collective of five nonprofit newsrooms. It draws on journalism best practices to raise the voices of the city’s youth and offer a fuller narrative of historically overlooked communities.

Follow Lisa Hamilton on LinkedIn

President and CEO Lisa Lawson gives followers a behind-the-scenes look at how Casey works to ensure America's children and young people have the opportunities they need to thrive.

Thank You For Your Interest

We hope you'll find value in this report. We’d love to get a little information from you, which we'll use to notify you about relevant new resources.

Please enter a valid email address

Subscribe to our newsletter to get our data, reports and news in your inbox.
